commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Turn away from man, whose breath is in his nostrils: for of what account is he. Adhere to Jesus Christ. (Origen) (Menochius) Septuagint omit this sentence, and St. Jerome thinks they did it perhaps for fear of shocking their brethren. In Jeremias xvii. It is supplied from Aquila's version, "how must he be esteemed. "(Calmet) Protestants, "for wherein is he to be accounted of. "Jesus will kill the wicked one with the spirit of his mouth, 2 Thessalonians ii. (Haydock) No dependence must be had in man. The Israelites vainly trusted in Egypt.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
